🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔍 PENDING\]

*Name:* Dunmore Pineapple
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* glasgow stirling
*County:* Falkirk
*Type:* castle
*Period:* Georgian (1761)
*Coords:* 56.07671, -3.7867
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dunmore_Pineapple
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Dunmore%20Pineapple%20Falkirk%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/Dunmore_pineapple.jpg/330px-Dunmore_pineapple.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Flag Reasons:* flagged-during-image-review
*Flag Note:* Auto-moved: insufficient images (0-1)
*Enrich Notes:* Not relevant to our site. Delete listing completely. 

*Description:*
The Dunmore Pineapple is a folly in Dunmore Park, near Airth in Stirlingshire, Scotland. In 1995, it was ranked "as the most bizarre building in Scotland".

_Listing 7 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Inverlochy Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* highlands
*County:* Highland
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 56.83258611, -5.08100278
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inverlochy_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Inverlochy%20Castle%20Highland%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNVC80H5GnzUgiuJB6Gl4HN9oiKYisRuXJmZN51L2vBA8IzuUVbVLI9MhKFKm5Lp_wdR_aG6hjxF1e7J9hxQERs4b5I6Zmuyxqh77mE034LGPIvYYInS8ducwre3z8ynazjo1OAHO7C_TDm42I=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* change name to Old Inverlochy Castle , google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/kVr3davEGAggDdSn9

*Description:*
Inverlochy Castle is a ruined, 13th-century castle near Inverlochy and Fort William, Highland, Scotland. The site of two battles, the castle remains largely unchanged since its construction. It is now in the care of Historic Environment Scotland.

_Listing 8 of 10_
